patch.value: CodeFactor/Maintainability: clear remaining unicorn lints (no-thenable ×4, no-useless-spread ×2, no-useless-length-check ×1)

Three small `eslint-plugin-unicorn` rule groups (7 findings total, Maintainability).

### 1. `no-thenable` ×4 — https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main?category=Maintainability&groupId=10592

A member named `then` makes an object look like a Promise (a "thenable") and can break `await`/Promise resolution. **Confirmed:** `src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts:2483` and `src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts:2493` (`then:` keys) — this is the main reason `cli-contracts.ts` is the #1 B-rated hotspot.

⚠️ **Risk:** `then` here is part of the CLI-contracts data surface. Before renaming, check whether the key is a public contract consumed by the SDK / snapshot and whether `pnpm contracts:check` or any consumer depends on it. May require a coordinated rename + snapshot regen, or a scoped CodeFactor ignore if the key is contractual.

### 2. `no-useless-spread` ×2 — https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main?category=Maintainability&groupId=10584

Redundant spread, e.g. `[...[a, b]]`, `{ ...{ a } }`, or `new Set([...iterable])` where the spread is unnecessary. Drop the spread.

### 3. `no-useless-length-check` ×1 — https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main?category=Maintainability&groupId=10597

Redundant `arr.length === 0 || arr.every(...)` (every() on empty is already true) or `arr.length > 0 && arr.some(...)` (some() on empty is already false). Drop the length check.

## Fix

#2 and #3 are mechanical/auto-fixable. #1 needs the contract-safety check above. Keep tests + 100% coverage + typecheck green.

CodeFactor reports 0 findings for no-thenable, no-useless-spread, and no-useless-length-check; the cli-contracts.ts `then` rename (if applied) does NOT break the contracts snapshot (pnpm contracts:check) or any SDK consumer; full coverage suite + typecheck green.
